title: Thin-file young applicants
text: Applicants under 25 with a short credit history age may be approved for entry-level products when income is stable and credit utilization is below 30%. A thin credit file alone is not grounds for decline; weight stable income and low delinquency more heavily than history length.

title: Credit utilization thresholds
text: Credit utilization above 35% is a concern and above 60% is a strong negative signal. Recommend utilization be reduced below 30%. Sustained high utilization with delayed payments should move a decision from Approve to Review.

title: Delayed payment handling
text: Up to two delayed payments may be tolerated for otherwise strong profiles. Three or more delayed payments require review and a recommendation to enable auto-pay. Recent delinquency is weighted more heavily than older delinquency.

title: Outstanding debt and DTI
text: Outstanding debt above 5,000 with high EMI relative to monthly in-hand salary indicates elevated debt-to-income. Recommend loan consolidation. Combine with utilization and delinquency signals before declining.

title: Income stability offset
text: Stable annual income and consistent monthly in-hand salary can offset a thin credit file or a single delinquency. Salaried occupations with predictable income are lower risk than variable-income profiles at the same score.

title: Anomaly and fraud signals
text: An anomaly score from the fraud service above threshold overrides a favorable credit score and routes the application to manual review. Mismatched identifiers or improbable income for occupation should be flagged, not auto-declined.
